title: commiphora enneaphylla
intro: Commiphora enneaphylla, also known as the African myrrh, is a type of plant species that belongs to the Burseraceae family, which is commonly found in Africa and the Middle East. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Commiphora enneaphylla include relief from pain and swelling, as well as its ability to treat skin conditions such as acne and wounds. In traditional medicine, Commiphora enneaphylla is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, ointments, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
